mechanism: Two adjacent walls are personified as making plans to meet at the corner where they physically join. The groan comes from a literal geometric fact presented as an ordinary rendezvous, not from language-specific wordplay.

en.setup: What did one wall say to the wall next to it?
en.combined: What did one wall say to the wall next to it? Let’s meet at the corner!
en.portability_notes: Makes adjacency explicit and preserves the physical/rendezvous double reading.

fr.setup: Qu’est-ce qu’un mur a dit au mur d’à côté ?
fr.combined: Qu’est-ce qu’un mur a dit au mur d’à côté ? On se retrouve au coin !
fr.portability_notes: Uses idiomatic conversational French; au coin supports a meeting place and a wall corner.

es.setup: ¿Qué le dijo una pared a la de al lado?
es.combined: ¿Qué le dijo una pared a la de al lado? ¡Nos vemos en el rincón!
es.portability_notes: Uses rincón for the inner corner formed by walls, directly repairing the panel-noted esquina seam.

ja.setup: 壁が隣の壁に何て言った？
ja.combined: 壁が隣の壁に何て言った？ 角で会おう！
ja.portability_notes: Uses 隣の壁 instead of stilted numeric wall counting and removes the parent’s unnecessary quotation marks.

revision_reason: Fresh majority-led synthesis: retain Gemini’s universal wall geometry, apply GPT’s concrete-physical lesson and Claude’s per-language idiom audit, change Spanish esquina to inner-corner rincón, and rewrite Japanese without numeric wall counting or quotation marks.
